rulings.Case Title: CHUN HONG LUO VS. BEAUTY BEAUTY USA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 3, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT CINDY CHEN'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT SHE DID NOT RECEIVE NOTICE OF THE SUMMONS OR COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEFEND THE ACTION AFTER RECEIVING A NOTICE OF DEFAULT. THE MOTION IS FURTHER UNTIMELY IF BROUGHT UNDER CCP SECTION 473.5. THE MOTION WOULD ALSO BE DENIED UNDER CCP SECTION 1008, MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION, AS UNTIMELY AND LACKING NEW FACTS OR CIRCUMSTANCES. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID HAWZ-MEN VS. BUCA RESTAURANTS 2, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Response To Request For Statement Of Damages And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 3, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT BUCA RESTAURANTS 2, INC.'S Motion To Compel Response To Request For Statement Of Damages And For Sanctions. THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS MOOT. HOWEVER, SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO DEFENDANT IN THE REASONABLE AMOUNT OF $410.00 FOR THE COSTS INCURRED IN BRINGING THIS MOTION. SEE ARGAME V. WERASOPHON (1997) 57 CAL.APP.4TH 616, 618. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID HAWZ-MEN VS. BUCA RESTAURANTS 2, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set No One And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 3, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT BUCA RESTAURANTS 2, INC.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set No One And For Sanctions. MATTER TO BE HEARD ON DISCOVERY CALENDAR, SAME DAY, AT 9:00AM. THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS MOOT. HOWEVER, SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO THE DEFENDANT IN THE REASONABLE AMOUNT OF $595.00 FOR FAILURE TO TIMELY RESPOND TO THE INTERROGATORIES (CCP SECTION 2023.010(d) AND FOR FAILURE TO CONFER (CCP SECTION 2023.020). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CONDENSA VS. MBV LAW, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Otice Of Petition And Second Petition To Comel Arbitration; Notice Of Lodgement Of Authorities; Second Petition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 3, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT MBV LAW, LLP, DON BUDER, PAUL NEIBERGS Second Petition To Comel Arbitration. DENIED. DEFENDANTS BRING A RENEWED MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH CCP SECTION 1008(b). DEFENDANTS DO NOT DEMONSTRATE DILIGENCE OR WHY THESE ISSUES WERE NOT PREVIOUSLY LITIGATED AND FAILS TO PROVIDE THE REQUIRED DECLARATION. THE MOTION ALSO FAILS ON ITS MERITS. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT SUING ON THE MERGER AGREEMENT. THEY ARE SUING BECAUSE DEFENDANTS ALLEGEDLY BREACHED THEIR INDEPENDENT ATTORNEY/CLIENT DUTY. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. VS. POLENG LOUNGE, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Appointment Of A Receiver And To Appoint Richard B. Cole As Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 3, 2010, line 2, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'S Motion For Appointment Of A Receiver And To Appoint Richard B. Cole As Receiver. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AARON KUMLIN et al VS. BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 3, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH, TIM RAIMER, AND STEVE KORCH'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD THE EXISTENCE OF A DUTY AND BREACH OF DUTY. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO REMAINDER. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AARON KUMLIN et al VS. BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 3, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH, TIM RAIMER, STEVE KORCH'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SEEK LEAVE OF THE COURT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 425.14. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HAIGHT ASHBURY FREE CLINICS, INC.,
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 3, 2010, line 6, RESPONDENT HAPPENING HOUSE VENTURES, DAVID SMITH MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. GRANTED. THE WORK PERFORMED WAS REASONABLE AND NECESSARY, AND THE BILLING RATES ARE COMMENSURATE WITH FEES CHARGED BY OTHER SAN FRANCISCO LAW FIRMS. DEFENDANT SMITH IS AWARDED $180,981 IN ATTORNEY'S FEES AND $6,471.56 IN COSTS. =(302/CWW)
